qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: TLS certificate validation unconditionally disabled in collateral fetching allows MITM attacks on attestation collateral
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable code: this finding covers disabled TLS certificate validation in collateral transport at src/collateral.rs, while the advisory covers missing cryptographic verification of QE Identity signature/certificate-chain policy and QE Report constraints.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded memory allocation in quote parsing allows denial of service via crafted binary input
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable code: this finding covers an unbounded Data<u32> allocation in Quote::decode/Quote::parse, not missing QE Identity signature verification or missing MRSIGNER, ISVPRODID, and ISVSVN checks against the QE Report.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described dcap-qvl root cause. The advisory concerns skipped verification of QE Identity signature against its issuer chain and missing QE Report policy checks for MRSIGNER, ISVPRODID, and ISVSVN; the qualifying findings instead cover disabled TLS validation during collateral fetching and unbounded allocation while parsing quote data.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-796p-j2gh-9m2q states that dcap-qvl fetches qe_identity collateral but skips verifying the QE Identity signature against its certificate chain and does not enforce QE Report policy constraints.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists the final qualifying findings as improper-tls-certificate-validation and unbounded-allocation-quote-parse.

improper-tls-certificate-validation/vulnerability_report.json identifies src/collateral.rs danger_accept_invalid_certs(true), a transport-layer TLS validation issue, and notes that verify() still performs cryptographic signature verification on collateral.

unbounded-allocation-quote-parse/vulnerability_report.json identifies src/quote.rs Data::<u32>::decode() allocating attacker-controlled lengths before bounds checks, which is unrelated to QE Identity signature or QE Report constraint verification.
